review_comment: Do you plan on reverting this after you found the bug, otherwise the assert does nothing since it always returns something.

I deleted the assert, ~~but maybe we should not extend the expire timestamp when the dict entry already exists. That can lead to a situation where we prolong the blacklisted nodes indefinitely~~. [Edit] It's OK to update the timestamp. The TTL of each blacklist entry is included in the ping extension so it will not be extended indefinitely when propagated multiple times though the cluster.
